title: Black Box Data After a Semi Crash in Houston, Texas
twitter: 🚨 Houston semi crash? The truck's black box data can be ERASED within days. Don't wait. Call 855 WRECKMATCH (855-897-3256) or get matched with a truck accident attorney now → https://www.wreckmatch.com/blog/black-box-data-after-a-semi-crash-in-houston-texas
linkedin:
If you handle truck accident litigation in Texas, black box data is often the difference between a strong case and a lost one — and that window closes fast.

After a semi crash in Houston, the truck's Event Data Recorder (EDR) and Engine Control Module (ECM) can capture critical pre-crash data: speed, braking, throttle position, hours of service, and more. But trucking companies are not legally obligated to preserve that data indefinitely. Without a timely spoliation letter or emergency preservation order, that evidence can be overwritten or destroyed within days.

For injured victims and their families, this timeline is unforgiving. Acting quickly — and connecting with an attorney who understands federal trucking regulations and Texas tort law — is essential.

WreckMatch works with a network of 800+ participating law firms across Texas and nationwide who handle serious truck accident cases. When someone is injured in a semi crash, we match them directly with attorneys equipped to move fast on evidence preservation.

If your firm handles truck accident cases or you know someone who needs help after a Houston semi crash, visit us at https://www.wreckmatch.com/#form to learn more or submit a case.
facebook:
After a semi-truck crash in Houston, one of the most important things people don't know is this: the truck's black box — also called an EDR or ECM — can be erased or overwritten within just a few days.

That data can show exactly how fast the truck was going, whether the driver braked, and how long they had been behind the wheel before the crash. It can be the most powerful evidence in your entire case.

But trucking companies know this too. Without quick legal action to preserve that data, it can simply disappear.

If you or someone you love was hurt in a collision with a semi-truck in the Houston area, time really does matter here. Getting connected with an experienced truck accident attorney as soon as possible could make all the difference in protecting your rights and your family's future.

Have you or someone you know ever had to deal with the aftermath of a serious truck accident? What was the hardest part of navigating the process?
reddit_title: Semi truck crash in Houston — does the black box data actually matter and how do you preserve it before it's gone?
reddit_body:
If you were recently in a crash involving a commercial semi-truck in Houston, the truck's onboard black box (technically the EDR or ECM) may contain some of the most valuable evidence in your case. It can record speed, braking behavior, throttle input, and even driver hours-of-service data in the moments before impact. The problem is that this data is not permanently stored — it can be overwritten by the truck's systems within days of the crash, especially if the truck returns to service.

The most important step is getting a spoliation letter sent to the trucking company as quickly as possible. This is a formal legal notice demanding that all electronic data, logs, and maintenance records be preserved. An attorney can also seek an emergency court order if necessary. Without this, there is no guarantee the data will still exist by the time litigation begins. Many victims lose this evidence simply because they didn't know to ask for it in time.

If you're trying to figure out your next steps after a Houston truck accident, connecting with an attorney who specifically handles commercial vehicle cases is critical — not just any personal injury lawyer. Resources like wreckmatch.com can match you with attorneys in their network who handle truck accident claims and understand the urgency around evidence preservation. Acting within the first 24 to 72 hours can genuinely change the outcome of your case.
